briefdescription.para: This macro selects the bridge YUV420 mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI RX Subsystem
enables or disables YUV420 mode

briefdescription.para: This macros selects the bridge pixel repeat mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI RX Subsystem
enables or disables pixel drop mode

briefdescription.para: Define Driver instance of all sub-core included in the design.
This function queries the subsystem instance configuration to determine the included sub-cores.

detaileddescription.para: For each sub-core that is present in the design the sub-core driver instance is binded with the subsystem sub-core driver handle

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
specifies device instance number

briefdescription.para: This function is the delay/sleep function for the XV_HdmiRxSs1 driver.

detaileddescription.para: For the Zynq family, there exists native sleep functionality. For MicroBlaze however, there does not exist such functionality. In the MicroBlaze case, the default method for delaying is to use a predetermined amount of loop iterations. This method is prone to inaccuracy and dependent on system configuration; for greater accuracy, the user may supply their own delay/sleep handler, pointed to by InstancePtr->UserTimerWaitUs, which may have better accuracy if a hardware timer is used.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HdmiSsRx instance.
is the number of microseconds to delay/sleep for.

briefdescription.para: This function retrieves the Vendor Specific Info Frame.
This function register's all sub-core ISR's with interrupt controller and any subsystem level call back function with requisite sub-core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when a RX connect event has occurred.
This function is called when a Bridge overflow event has occurred.
This function is called when a RX AUX IRQ has occurred.
This function is called when a RX Audio IRQ has occurred.
This function is called when a RX Link Status IRQ has occurred.
This function is called when a RX DDC IRQ has occurred.
This function is called when the RX stream is down.
This function is called when the RX stream init .
This function is called when the RX stream is up.
This function is called when a RX Sync Loss IRQ has occurred.
This function is called when the mode has transitioned from DVI to HDMI or vice versa.
This function is called when the TMDS CLK ratio changes.
This function is called when the RX Phy needs to be reset.
This function is called when the Vic does not match with the detected video timing.
This function is called when the Link Ready error happens.
This function is called when the Video Ready error happens.
This function is called when the Skew Lock error happens.
This function is called internally to handle DSC stream change events.

detaileddescription.para: It logs the event if logging is enabled and then invokes the user-registered DSC stream change event callback, if one is set.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the HDMI RX Subsystem instance (XV_HdmiRxSs1 *).

simplesect.para: The user must register a callback function using the appropriate API to receive DSC stream change notifications.

briefdescription.para: This function logs the DSC packet error event if logging is enabled, and then calls the user-registered DSC packet error callback, if it is set.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance passed as callback reference.

simplesect.para: This function is intended for internal use by the HDMI RX Subsystem driver.

briefdescription.para: This function is invoked when a DSC status update event occurs.

detaileddescription.para: It logs the event if logging is enabled and then calls the registered DSC status update event callback, if one is set.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the HDMI RX Subsystem instance (XV_HdmiRxSs1).

simplesect.para: This function is intended for internal use within the HDMI RX Subsystem driver.

briefdescription.para: This function configures the Bridge for YUV420 functionality and repeater.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function prints detailed information about the HDMI RX Subsystem, including core info, timing, link quality, audio, HDR metadata, and InfoFrames.

detaileddescription.para: Report subsystem info.

parameterdescription.para: Instance Pointer to the main data structure

briefdescription.para: This function prints out the sub-core register dump.

detaileddescription.para: Display register contents.

parameterdescription.para: Instance pointer to the main data structure.

briefdescription.para: This function reports the list of cores included in the HDMI RX Subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: Report core info.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI RX Subsystem instance.

briefdescription.para: This function allows the user to specify a custom delay or sleep function, which will be invoked by the driver when a delay is required.

detaileddescription.para: Set user timer handler.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance.
is the address of the custom delay/sleep callback function.
is the user data (e.g., microseconds to delay) that will be passed to the callback function.

briefdescription.para: This function calls the interrupt handler for HDMI RX.

detaileddescription.para: HDMI RX interrupt handler.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI RX Subsystem

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the video subsystem and included sub-cores.

detaileddescription.para: Initialize subsystem.

This function must be called prior to using the subsystem. Initialization includes setting up the instance data for top level as well as all included sub-core therein, and ensuring the hardware is in a known stable state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
points to the configuration structure associated with the subsystem instance.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if initialization is successful else XST_FAILURE

briefdescription.para: This function starts the HDMI RX subsystem including all sub-cores that are included in the processing pipeline for a given use-case.

detaileddescription.para: Start subsystem.

Video pipe is started from back to front
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
None
Cores are started only if the corresponding start flag in the scratch pad memory is set. This allows to selectively start only those cores included in the processing chain

briefdescription.para: This function stops the HDMI RX subsystem including all sub-cores.

detaileddescription.para: Stop subsystem.
Stop the video pipe starting from front to back.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the video subsystem sub-cores.

detaileddescription.para: Reset subsystem.

There are 2 reset networks within the subsystem
For cores that are on AXIS interface
For cores that are on AXI-MM interface

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function controls the internal video reset signal for the HDMI subcore inside the subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: RX core video reset - TRUE to assert, FALSE to release.
It can either assert (activate) or release (deactivate) the reset based on the value of the Reset parameter.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is the value indicating whether to assert (non-zero) or release (zero) the reset.

briefdescription.para: This function asserts or releases the Internal Link reset of the HDMI subcore within the subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: RX core link reset - TRUE to assert, FALSE to release.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either assert or release reset.

briefdescription.para: This function asserts or releases the video reset of other blocks within the subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: Video reset - TRUE to assert, FALSE to release.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either assert or release reset.

briefdescription.para: This function asserts or releases the system reset of other blocks within the subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: System reset - TRUE to assert, FALSE to release.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is the value indicating whether to assert (non-zero) or release (zero) the reset.

briefdescription.para: This function installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

detaileddescription.para: Set event callback.

para.preformatted: HandlerType Callback Function Type ----------------------- --------------------------------------------- (XV_HDMIRXSS1_HANDLER_CONNECT) HpdCallback (XV_HDMIRXSS1_HANDLER_VS) VsCallback (XV_HDMIRXSS1_HANDLER_STREAM_DOWN) StreamDownCallback (XV_HDMIRXSS1_HANDLER_STREAM_UP) StreamUpCallback (XV_HDMIRXSS1_HANDLER_HDCP_AUTHENTICATED) HdcpAuthenticatedCallback (XV_HDMIRXSS1_HANDLER_HDCP_UNAUTHENTICATED) HdcpUnauthenticatedCallback (XV_HDMIRXSS1_HANDLER_HDCP_AUTHENTICATION_REQUEST) HdcpAuthenticationRequestCallback (XV_HDMIRXSS1_HANDLER_HDCP_STREAM_MANAGE_REQUEST) HdcpStreamManageRequestCallback (XV_HDMIRXSS1_HANDLER_HDCP_TOPOLOGY_UPDATE) HdcpTopologyUpdateCallback

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI RX Subsystem instance.
specifies the type of handler.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if callback function installed successfully.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This function installs an asynchronous callback function for the LogWrite API:

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI RX Subsystem instance.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if callback function installed successfully.
XST_INVALID_PARAM when callback function is not installed successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function Sets the EDID parameters in the HDMI RX SS struct.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI RX Subsystem instance.
is a pointer to EDID data
specifies the length of EDID data

briefdescription.para: This function loads the default EDID to the HDMI RX.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI RX Subsystem instance.

briefdescription.para: This function loads the default EDID to the HDMI RX.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI RX Subsystem instance.
is a pointer to EDID data
specifies the length of EDID data

briefdescription.para: This function sets the HPD on the HDMI RX.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI RX Subsystem instance.

is a flag used to set the HPD.
TRUE drives HPD high
FALSE drives HPD low

briefdescription.para: This function toggles the HPD on the HDMI RX.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI RX Subsystem instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI RX SS Aux structure.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI RX SS AVI InfoFrame structure.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRXSs instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI RX SS General Control Packet structure.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRXSs instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI RX SS Audio InfoFrame structure.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRXSs instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI 2.1 RX SS DRM InfoFrame structure.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI RX SS Vendor Specific InfoFrame structure.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function set HDMI RX subsystem stream parameters.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance
specifies reference pixel clock frequency.
specifies FRL linerate

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI RX SS video stream.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI RX SS video Identification code.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI RX SS video stream type.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI RX SS video stream type.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to HDMI RX SS video stream type.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the HDMI RX SS number of active audio channels.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the HDMI RX SS audio format.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the HDMI RX SS CTS value.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function returns the HDMI RX SS CTS value.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function is called when HDMI RX SS TMDS clock changes.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function prints debug information on STDIO/UART console.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance.

briefdescription.para: This function prints out RX's SCDC registers and values on STDIO/UART console.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance.

briefdescription.para: This function prints the HDMI RX SS timing information.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function reports the link quality based on the link error counter.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function prints the HDMI 2.1 RX SS static HDR infoframe information.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function prints the HDMI RX SS audio information.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function prints the HDMI RX SS audio information.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function prints the HDMI RX SS subcore versions.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the video stream is up.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the interface is connected.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance

listitem.para: FALSE if interface is not connected.

briefdescription.para: This function will set the default in HDF.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs1 core instance.
is the XV_HdmiRxSs1 ID to operate on.

briefdescription.para: This function will set PPC specified by user.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs1 core instance.
is the XV_HdmiRxSs1 ID to operate on.
is the PPC to be set.

briefdescription.para: This function will set the major and minor application version in RXSs struct.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs1 core instance.
is the major version of the application.
is the minor version of the application.

briefdescription.para: This function set HDMI RX audio parameters.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs1 core instance.
0: Unmute the audio 1: Mute the audio.

briefdescription.para: This function controls HDMI RX VFP event enable/disable.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs1 core instance.
0: disable VFP event 1: enable VFP event.

briefdescription.para: This function returns VRR infoframe type.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns core ppc value.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRXSs instance

briefdescription.para: This function enables the Data mover in Dynamic HDR.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRXSs instance

briefdescription.para: This function disables the Data mover in Dynamic HDR.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRXSs instance

briefdescription.para: This function sets the buffer address for Dynamic HDR.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRXSs instance
64 bit address

briefdescription.para: This function sets the buffer address for Dynamic HDR.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRXSs instance
pointer to dynamic hdr info structure. This is passed by the application.

briefdescription.para: This function verifies whether the DSC feature is enabled in the HDMI RX subsystem and, if so, queries the underlying HDMI RX core to determine if the DSC stream is active.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_HdmiRxSs1 instance.

listitem.para: 1 if the DSC stream is enabled.
0 if the DSC stream is not enabled or DSC is not supported.

simplesect.para: The function asserts that both InstancePtr and InstancePtr->HdmiRx1Ptr are valid.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the DSC Decode Fail flag in the HDMI RX FRL DDC field.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRXSs instance

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the operation was successful.
XST_NO_FEATURE if DSC is not supported in the current configuration

simplesect.para: This function uses an assertion to ensure that the input pointer is not NULL.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the DSC FRL Max flag in the HDMI RX FRL DDC field.

detaileddescription.para: This function writes a value of 1 to the DSC_FRL_MAX field using the FRL DDC interface. It is used to indicate that the maximum FRL (Fixed Rate Link) bandwidth is being used for Display Stream Compression (DSC) on the HDMI RX path

parameterdescription.para: pointer to XV_HdmiRXSs instance

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the operation was successful.
XST_NO_FEATURE if DSC is not supported in the current configuration

simplesect.para: This function uses an assertion to ensure that the input pointer is not NULL.

briefdescription.para: This is main code of Xilinx HDMI Receiver Subsystem device driver.

detaileddescription.para: Please see xv_hdmirxss1.h for more details of the driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 EB 22/05/18 Initial release.
